stories.title: Anthropic updates Claude voice mode with more capable models
stories.simple_summary: Anthropic has improved Claude, their AI assistant, so it now works better when you talk to it by voice. It can help you reschedule meetings or write emails by listening to what you say.
stories.why_it_matters: If you use voice assistants and want one that understands speech better, this is a small improvement — but most small-business owners won't notice a big difference yet.

stories.title: Claude’s voice mode is now available for Opus and Sonnet
stories.simple_summary: Anthropic is adding voice mode to two of their smarter AI models (Opus and Sonnet) and making it work with popular tools like Gmail and Slack.
stories.why_it_matters: If you already use AI assistants at work, this means more options for talking to them instead of typing — but it's mostly a convenience upgrade.

stories.title: AegisAI, founded by former Google security execs, lands $36M to stop AI-driven spear phishing
stories.simple_summary: A new company called AegisAI, founded by former Google security experts, has received $36 million to build AI tools that catch fake phishing emails — messages that trick people into revealing passwords or information.
stories.why_it_matters: If you run a business, this could eventually help protect your employees from email scams, though this news is mainly for big companies right now.

stories.title: Patreon is laying off 20 percent of workers
stories.simple_summary: Patreon, a website where creators get paid by fans, is firing about 93 employees because AI has changed how the company works.
stories.why_it_matters: This doesn't directly affect you unless you're a Patreon creator, but it shows many companies are using AI to do work that people used to do.

stories.title: Runway launches AI model router as generative media gets crowded
stories.simple_summary: Runway, a company that makes AI video and image tools, has created a new system that automatically picks the fastest or cheapest AI model for whatever job you need done.
stories.why_it_matters: This is mainly for software developers and companies making creative tools — nothing you need to worry about as a small-business owner.

stories.title: OpenAI makes ChatGPT Health available to all US users
stories.simple_summary: OpenAI has made ChatGPT Health available to everyone in the US, so you can connect it to your health data from apps like Apple Health and fitness trackers.
stories.why_it_matters: If you want an AI tool to help you understand your health and fitness information, this is now available, but remember it's not a replacement for a real doctor.

stories.title: OpenAI is making big claims as it rolls out ChatGPT Health to everyone
stories.simple_summary: OpenAI is telling people that its AI can now reason about health questions as well as or better than doctors.
stories.why_it_matters: These are big claims that haven't been proven yet — be cautious about relying on AI for serious health decisions instead of seeing an actual doctor.

stories.title: Meta launched a new AI optimism ad set to a song about human extinction
stories.simple_summary: Meta made an advertisement about how great AI is, but they used a famous song that's actually about the world ending in five years.
stories.why_it_matters: This is just a funny mistake Meta made in their advertising — it doesn't affect you, but shows they weren't thinking carefully about their message.

stories.title: Nvidia is sending GPUs to the moon
stories.simple_summary: Nvidia is sending computer chips called GPUs (the processors that power AI) to the moon.
stories.why_it_matters: This is interesting news for space exploration, but it's mainly a story about Nvidia's business — nothing that changes your life.

stories.title: AI chip startup Etched defies skeptics, hits $10.3B valuation from big-name investors
stories.simple_summary: A new AI chip company called Etched has raised money and is now worth over $10 billion because they claim to have made chips that work faster than the ones Nvidia sells.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors — unless you run a huge company that uses lots of AI, this competition between chip makers won't affect you.

stories.title: Google’s Gemini nears billion-user milestone
stories.simple_summary: Google's Gemini AI tool now has over 750 million monthly users and is getting close to a billion.
stories.why_it_matters: This shows how popular AI assistants are becoming, but it's mainly a business milestone — nothing new for you to use.

stories.title: Lawmakers prepare bill requiring AI ‘kill switch’
stories.simple_summary: Some lawmakers are preparing a new law that would let the government shut down or slow down AI systems in emergencies, similar to how they can control other dangerous things.
stories.why_it_matters: This is about government regulation of AI — if passed, it could eventually make AI companies more responsible, but it's too early to know if or when this would actually happen.
